# Create an alias suffix for a scope

When you create an alias in a project scope, you must create suffixes for both the containing org scope and the project scope in which the alias resides. You can have only one suffix per project or org scope. Org suffixes must be globally unique. Project suffixes must be unique within the org the project resides in, but they can be reused across orgs.

Once you create the suffixes, you append them to any aliases you create in the project scope. You can reuse alias values across scopes because the suffixes make them globally unique values.

For example, your organization might use an alias value `postgres-db` and differentiate between different departments using suffixes. An engineering org might use the suffix `eng` and suffixes `frontend` and `backend` for projects within the org scope to create the following alias combinations:

- `postgres-db` - Alias in the global scope
- `postgres-db.backend.eng` - Alias in the project scope with org and project suffixes
- `postgres-db.frontend.eng` - Alias in the project scope with org and project suffixes

## Permissions

@include '/suffixes/permissions.mdx'

## Create the suffixes

Complete the following steps to create suffixes for the org and project scopes:

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="Admin Console" group="admin">

Select whether you want to create a suffix for an org or a project scope:

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="Org">

1. Log in to Boundary.
1. Select **Orgs** in the navigation pane, and then select the org scope.
1. Select **Org Settings** in the navigation pane, and then click **Create alias suffix**.
1. In the **Alias suffix** field, type a unique value for the suffix.
1. Click **Save**.

  Boundary will append the org suffix to any aliases you create in the nested project scope using the following format:

  `alias.projectsuffix.orgsuffix`

</Tab>

<Tab heading="Project">

1. Log in to Boundary.
1. Select **Orgs** in the navigation pane, and then select the org scope that contains the project.
1. Select the project scope.
1. Select **Project Settings** in the navigation pane, and then click **Create alias suffix**.
1. In the **Alias suffix** field, type a unique value for the suffix.
1. Click **Save**.

  Boundary will append the project suffix to any aliases you create in the project scope using the following format:

  `alias.projectsuffix.orgsuffix`

</Tab>
</Tabs>

</Tab>

<Tab heading="CLI" group="cli">

Select whether you want to create a suffix for an org or a project scope:

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="Org">

1. Log in to Boundary.
1. Use the following command to create an alias suffix in the org scope that contains the project scope. Make sure to provide the ID for the org scope:

   ```shell-session
   $ boundary scopes set-alias-suffix \
       -id o_1234567890 \
       -alias-suffix org1
   ```

  You must provide the the following attributes when you create a suffix:

  @include 'scopes/set-alias-suffix.mdx'

  When you create an alias in the project scope, you must append the suffix using the following format:

  `alias.projectsuffix.orgsuffix`

</Tab>
<Tab heading="Project">

1. Log in to Boundary.
1. Use the following command to create an alias suffix in the project scope you want to create an alias for. Make sure to provide the ID for the project scope:

   ```shell-session
   $ boundary scopes set-alias-suffix \
       -id p_1234567890 \
       -alias-suffix proj1
   ```

  You must provide the the following attributes when you create a suffix:

  @include 'scopes/set-alias-suffix.mdx'

  When you create an alias in the project scope, you must append the suffix using the following format:

  `alias.projectsuffix.orgsuffix`

</Tab>
</Tabs>

</Tab>
</Tabs>

## Next steps

Once you have created suffixes for the org and project scope, you can [Create a target alias](/boundary/docs/targets/configuration/create-target-alias) for targets in the project scope.

## More information

- For more information about aliases, refer to [Overview of aliases](/boundary/docs/targets/configuration).
- To change the name of a suffix or to delete it, refer to [Manage alias suffixes](/boundary/docs/targets/configuration/manage-suffix).
